conversations.say: Arden, let's look past the anniversary ceremony. I have the original First R Letter packet right here. If Veridian's intake report and expense records are as period-consistent as claimed, let's put them side by side before anyone spins a new myth.
conversations.offer: Voluntary viewing of the First R Letter packet
conversations.request: Place Veridian's intake report and expense record beside the letter for comparison

conversations.say: Jules, you want succession recognized, but a grand title without scrutiny is just another press release. Show me why your message deserves the mantle and how it withstands the doubts Vale planted.
conversations.offer: My comparison between the letter's language and your succession message
conversations.request: Explain why your succession deserves recognition without first asking for a blind blessing

conversations.say: Tessa, you've always argued that celebrated bylines erase collective labor. I want to hear directly from you which parts of my old newsroom stories papered over the work of researchers and assistants.
conversations.offer: Early newsroom history and my perspective on how editing shaped early R
conversations.request: Tell me honestly which parts of my old newsroom stories erased other people's work

public_statement: Fifteen years ago, we recognized a singular voice on the metro desk because it cut through the noise. If people now want to debate whether that beginning was a solitary person or a manufactured legend, let's start with the facts we actually hold rather than hiding behind absent friends.

commitments.promise: I will share my physical First R Letter packet for comparison if Arden provides the Veridian intake report and expense record.

goal_progress: Asked Arden to examine the letter packet alongside Veridian's intake report and expense record.
Invited Jules to justify succession and address the language similarities in the message.
Engaged Tessa regarding how newsroom authorship and bylines mask collective labor.
reasoning_summary: In Section 1, Frankie Lowell defends the integrity of his origin story while confronting the unsettling questions raised by Vale's absence and message V2M003. He opens dialogues with Arden Bell to examine records, Jules Kwan to test succession claims, and Tessa Quill to examine collective labor versus individual bylines, establishing his grounded yet vulnerable stance.
